author: snuffled
text:
Land the nested-XDG layout foundation: encoding, paths, four-file config

Phase 1 of bl-f021 split into two slices; this is the foundation half. Adds seven pure modules every later integration slice rests on. No behavior change for existing clones — nothing wires into Store::discover, bl init, or bl claim yet. The integration slice (Store::discover rewrite + dual-read + init/claim relocation) ships as bl-203e.

Modules: encoding (URL canonicalize + percent-encode + nested clone path, frozen golden vectors per SPEC §14.2); xdg_paths (flat per-host layout under ~/.local/state/balls/ — no hashing, no view symlinks); tracker_json (strict pointer-only schema, presence is the §5 bootstrap signal); layered_fields (Integrate / IntegrateMode / ReviewBlock with the §6.6 renames, explicitly rejecting legacy field values); repo_json (per-code-repo, rejects tracker-scope fields per §6.5 and the removed target_branch per §6.7); clone_json (per-on-disk-checkout, owns stealth/tasks_dir, retires the prior 'synthetic origin-key' fallback); effective_config (clone ?? repo ?? project ?? default precedence, the single seam for layered fields).

ProjectConfig gains the five §6.2 second-block optional layered defaults — Option<>-typed so the merger can distinguish 'set' from 'fall through.' Existing project.json files round-trip byte-identically (skip_serializing_if = None).

Coverage 100%, clippy clean, every new file under the 300-line cap. SPEC §14 conformance: §14.2 (encoding), §14.6 (tracker.json strict), §14.8 (three-way precedence), §14.9 (tracker-scope rejection), §14.16/§14.17 (new field-name acceptance + legacy-name rejection on freshly-written files) all asserted via module-level tests.
